Package org.rodinp.core

Interface IRodinElement

All Superinterfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable
All Known Subinterfaces:
IAction, IAssignmentElement, IAxiom, ICarrierSet, ICommentedElement, IConfigurationElement, IConstant, IContextRoot, IContextRoot, IConvergenceElement, IData, IDependency, IDerivedPredicateElement, IElementManipulation, IEvent, IEventBRoot, IExpressionElement, IExtendsContext, IGuard, IIdentifierElement, IInternalElement, IInvariant, ILabeledElement, ILanguage, IMachineRoot, IMachineRoot, IParameter, IPOHint, IPOIdentifier, IPOPredicate, IPOPredicateSet, IPORoot, IPORoot, IPOSelectionHint, IPOSequent, IPOSource, IPredicateElement, IPRExprRef, IPRHypAction, IPRIdentifier, IPRPredRef, IPRProof, IPRProofRule, IPRReasoner, IPRRoot, IPRRuleAntecedent, IPRStoredExpr, IPRStoredPred, IPRStringInput, IPSRoot, IPSStatus, IReference, IRefinesEvent, IRefinesMachine, IRodinDB, IRodinFile, IRodinProject, ISCAction, ISCAssignmentElement, ISCAxiom, ISCCarrierSet, ISCConstant, ISCContext, ISCContextRoot, ISCContextRoot, ISCEvent, ISCExpressionElement, ISCExtendsContext, ISCGuard, ISCIdentifierElement, ISCInternalContext, ISCInvariant, ISCMachineRoot, ISCMachineRoot, ISCParameter, ISCPredicateElement, ISCProvableRoot, ISCRefinesEvent, ISCRefinesMachine, ISCSeesContext, ISCVariable, ISCVariant, ISCWitness, ISeesContext, ISnapshotable, ITestChildElement, ITestParentElement, ITraceableElement, IVariable, IVariant, IVersionEA, IVersionEB, IVersionEC, IVersionRootA, IVersionRootB, IVersionRootC, IVersionRootD, IVersionRootE, IVersionRootF, IVersionRootG, IVersionRootH, IWitness
All Known Implementing Classes:
Action, Axiom, CarrierSet, ComponentRoot, Constant, ContextRoot, ContextRoot, Data, Dependency, Event, EventBElement, EventBPOElement, EventBProofElement, EventBRoot, ExtendsContext, Guard, ImplicitHolder, InternalElement, Invariant, Language, MachineRoot, MachineRoot, NamedElement, NamedElement2, Openable, Parameter, POIdentifier, POPredicate, POPredicateSet, PORoot, PORoot, POSelectionHint, POSequent, POSource, PRExprRef, PRHypAction, PRIdentifier, PRPredRef, PRProof, PRProofRule, PRReasoner, PRRoot, PRRuleAntecedent, PRStoredExpr, PRStoredPred, PRStringInput, PSRoot, PSStatus, Reference, RefinesEvent, RefinesMachine, RodinDB, RodinElement, RodinFile, RodinProject, RodinTestDependency, RodinTestRoot, RodinTestRoot, RodinTestRoot2, SCAction, SCAssignmentElement, SCAxiom, SCCarrierSet, SCConstant, SCContextRoot, SCContextRoot, SCEvent, SCExpressionElement, SCExtendsContext, SCGuard, SCIdentifierElement, SCInternalContext, SCInvariant, SCMachineRoot, SCMachineRoot, SCParameter, SCPredicateElement, SCRefinesEvent, SCRefinesMachine, SCSeesContext, SCVariable, SCVariant, SCWitness, SeesContext, TestChildElement, TestParentElement, UbiquitousElement, Variable, Variant, VersionEA, VersionEB, VersionEC, VersionRootA, VersionRootB, VersionRootC, VersionRootD, VersionRootE, VersionRootF, VersionRootG, VersionRootH, Witness
public interface IRodinElement extends org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable
Common protocol for all elements provided by the Rodin database. Rodin database elements are exposed to clients as handles to the actual underlying element. The Rodin database may hand out any number of handles for each element. Handles that refer to the same element are guaranteed to be equal, but not necessarily identical.

Methods annotated as "handle-only" do not require underlying elements to exist. Methods that require underlying elements to exist throw a RodinDBException when an underlying element is missing. RodinDBException.isDoesNotExist() can be used to recognize this common special case.

Since:
1.0

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    createProblemMarker(IRodinProblem problem, Object... args)
    Creates a new Rodin Problem marker for this element.

    Returns the first ancestor of this Rodin element that has the given type.

    Returns the name of this element.

    Returns the type of this element.

    Returns a string representation of this element handle.
    IOpenable

    Returns the first openable parent.
    IRodinElement

    Returns the element directly containing this element, or null if this element has no parent.

    Returns the path to the innermost resource enclosing this element.

    Returns the innermost resource enclosing this element.

    Returns the Rodin database.
    IRodinProject

    Returns the Rodin project this element is contained in, or null if this element is not contained in any Rodin project (for instance, the IRodinDB is not contained in any Rodin project).

    getSchedulingRule()
    Returns the scheduling rule associated with this Rodin element.

    Returns the smallest underlying resource that contains this element, or null if this element is not contained in a resource.
    boolean
    isAncestorOf(IRodinElement element)
    Returns whether this element is an ancestor of the given element.
    boolean
    isReadOnly()
    Returns whether this Rodin element is read-only.
    boolean
    isRoot()
    Returns whether this Rodin element is a root element.

    Methods inherited from interface org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable

    getAdapter

  • Method Details

    • createProblemMarker

      void createProblemMarker(IRodinProblem problem, Object... args) throws RodinDBException
      Creates a new Rodin Problem marker for this element.

      The new marker is attached to the underlying resource of this element. Its marker type is RodinMarkerUtil.RODIN_PROBLEM_MARKER.

      Parameters:
      problem - problem to attach to the new marker
      args - arguments to the problem
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this method fails. Reasons include:
      • This element does not exist.
      See Also:
      • RodinMarkerUtil

    • exists

      boolean exists()
      Returns whether this Rodin element exists in the database.

      Rodin elements are handle objects that may or may not be backed by an actual element. Rodin elements that are backed by an actual element are said to "exist", and this method returns true. For Rodin elements, it is always the case that if the element exists, then its parent also exists (provided it has one). Moreover, for any element which is not a stable file snapshot, the parent includes the element as one of its children. It is therefore possible to navigate to any existing Rodin element (except stable file snapshots) from the root of the Rodin database along a chain of existing Rodin elements. On the other hand, a stable snapshot file never shows up among the children of its parent element.

      The fact that an openable element exists gives no guarantee upon whether it can be opened. For instance, a malformed Rodin file may exist, while it can never be opened by the database (because it contains invalid XML).

      A call to this method might open the ancestors of this element, but will never open this element itself.

      Returns:
      true if this element exists in the Rodin database, and false if this element does not exist

    • getAncestor

      <T extends IRodinElement> T getAncestor(IElementType<T> ancestorType)
      Returns the first ancestor of this Rodin element that has the given type. Returns null if no such an ancestor can be found.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Parameters:
      ancestorType - the type of the ancestor to look up
      Returns:
      the first ancestor of this Rodin element that has the given type, or null if no such an ancestor can be found

    • getCorrespondingResource

      org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource getCorrespondingResource()
      Returns the resource that corresponds directly to this element, or null if there is no resource that corresponds to this element.

      For example, the corresponding resource for an IRodinFile is its underlying IFile. The corresponding resource for an IRodinProject is its underlying IProject. Internal elements have no corresponding resource.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the corresponding resource, or null if none

    • getElementName

      String getElementName()
      Returns the name of this element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the element name

    • getElementType

      IElementType<? extends IRodinElement> getElementType()
      Returns the type of this element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the element type

    • getHandleIdentifier

      String getHandleIdentifier()
      Returns a string representation of this element handle. The format of the string is not specified; however, the identifier is stable across workspace sessions, and can be used to recreate this handle via the RodinCore.valueOf(String) method.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the string handle identifier
      See Also:
      • RodinCore.valueOf(String)

    • getRodinDB

      IRodinDB getRodinDB()
      Returns the Rodin database.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the Rodin database

    • getRodinProject

      IRodinProject getRodinProject()
      Returns the Rodin project this element is contained in, or null if this element is not contained in any Rodin project (for instance, the IRodinDB is not contained in any Rodin project).

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the containing Rodin project, or null if this element is not contained in a Rodin project

    • getOpenable

      IOpenable getOpenable()
      Returns the first openable parent. If this element is openable, the element itself is returned. Returns null if this element doesn't have an openable parent.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the first openable parent or null if this element doesn't have an openable parent.

    • getParent

      IRodinElement getParent()
      Returns the element directly containing this element, or null if this element has no parent.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the parent element, or null if this element has no parent

    • getPath

      org.eclipse.core.runtime.IPath getPath()
      Returns the path to the innermost resource enclosing this element. The path returned is the full, absolute path to the underlying resource, relative to the workspace.

      This is a handle-only method.

      This is a convenience method fully equivalent to getOpenable().getFullPath().

      Returns:
      the path to the innermost resource enclosing this element

    • getResource

      org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource getResource()
      Returns the innermost resource enclosing this element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the innermost resource enclosing this element

    • getSchedulingRule

      org.eclipse.core.runtime.jobs.ISchedulingRule getSchedulingRule()
      Returns the scheduling rule associated with this Rodin element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the scheduling rule associated with this Rodin element

    • getUnderlyingResource

      org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource getUnderlyingResource()
      Returns the smallest underlying resource that contains this element, or null if this element is not contained in a resource.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the underlying resource, or null if none

    • isAncestorOf

      boolean isAncestorOf(IRodinElement element)
      Returns whether this element is an ancestor of the given element. This method implements the strict relationship: an element is not considered an ancestor of itself.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Parameters:
      element - the element to test as a descendent
      Returns:
      true iff this element is an ancestor of the given element

    • isReadOnly

      boolean isReadOnly()
      Returns whether this Rodin element is read-only. An element can be read-only for two reasons: either it corresponds to a resource which is read-only, or it is an internal element that belongs to a stable snapshot of a Rodin file, or the stable snapshot itself.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      true iff this element is read-only

    • isRoot

      boolean isRoot()
      Returns whether this Rodin element is a root element. A root element is an internal element which is the child of a non-internal element (for instance, the top-level element of a Rodin file).

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      true iff this element is a root element
